title: Introducing monday AI assistant! 👨‍💻
excerpt:
Get ready for a new era of productivity. We will be bringing the power of Artificial intelligence to your workspaces and are opening up our platform for third-party developers to build AI apps on the monday marketplace. 
…

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Get ready for a new era of productivity. We will be bringing the power of Artificial intelligence to your workspaces and are opening up our platform for third-party developers to build AI apps on the monday marketplace.

In May, we will be launching a beta version of monday AI assistant which will include a collection of innovative apps built internally on the same monday AI assistant infrastructure where external developers will be able to build apps for the monday marketplace.

As part of the beta launch, monday AI assistant will be able to:

Generate tasks

AI assistant _ Projects - EN1920×1080 94.7 KB

Compose and rephrase emails

AI assistent _ CRM - EN1920×1080 88.4 KB

Summarize complex tasks

AI assistant _ DOC - EN1920×1080 113 KB

Build formulas

AI assistant _ Formula - EN1920×1080 100 KB

With this launch, monday.com will become one of the first SaaS companies to allow external developers to add further functionalities.

Interested in being one of the first app developers to develop the power of artificial intelligence into monday workspaces? monday.com will host an external hackathon from June 20-22, offering an in-person experience to developers in London, New York and Tel Aviv, and an opportunity to participate virtually to developers around the world.

See more information here.

What will be your first command to your monday AI assistant?

posts.author: OrFrid (Or Fridman)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi community!
I am Or, a product lead at monday. As we prepare to bring the power of artificial intelligence to your workspaces, we want to invite the community to become part of our beta testers.
Sign up here to join us and be part of bringing AI to the platform.

posts.cookedHtml: So AI assistant is something that haven’t been developed yet? You offers to developers to start migrate their AI apps to the marketplace? Just like a regular monday marketplace app?

posts.author: OrFrid (Or Fridman)

posts.author: OrFrid (Or Fridman)
posts.cookedHtml:
AI assistant is something that will be gradually released soon (so you can understand it was developed and worked nicely ;)).
We offer developers to add more capabilities on this infrastructure, so users will be able to consume them also through the monday marketplace.
Let me know if you have any questions on that!
Or

posts.author: MrAwesome (Benjamin Walsh)
posts.cookedHtml:
Quick question:

Does the AI store the data is scans in a repository somewhere, and will it be using that data to answer questions for others who use Monday AI Assistant?

posts.author: Usernamegame (Jake N)
posts.cookedHtml: @Julietteb @OrFrid @BiancaT What if Monday.com focused on other things that it is shockingly bad at vs developing something with the letters “AI” so that you can mention it on earnings calls? Get the basics of project management right! Where are recurring tasks that dont take some “Automation” to use? How about cross board dependencies? How about you incorporate the things that we have to pay other apps, like generalcaster and autoboost, to do which your platform should do natively? How about fixing obvious things first, like being able to use mirror columns and formula columns like actual columns? The list goes on. I have spent DAYS. DAYS! trying to figure out how to make this software do something simple for a standard project management application!!! Maybe Monday.com should use some AI model to comb through its community board to see how let down its users are about the platform’s failure to do basic things? You are a >$5B market cap company (even after it dropped 60%)!!! Get a line of credit and hire people to fix the basic stuff!!!, Yours truly, Frustrated in Atlanta & looking at alternatives.

posts.author: Agueropez (Julio Aguero)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi. Can Ai Assistant work in Spanish?

posts.author: TFaust (Timo Faust)
posts.cookedHtml:
Is this an AI that has been developed by Monday.com or has it been acquired by a 3rd party?
How is data protection handled when we use the AI for processing data? Is the data solely managed within Monday.com or is data shared with a 3rd party provider?
If so, where can we read about GDPR conformity?

These points were not at all mentioned and answered in yesterdays presentation of new features, so I hope to get some answers this way.

Thanks in advance!

posts.author: OrFrid (Or Fridman)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @TFaust !
Thanks for your questions and interest in monday AI!
A few responses:

  1. monday.com is using 3rd party vendors as our models for the different features.
  2. Take a look on our AI terms & conditions that I believe address your questions regarding the data: monday AI Beta Terms and Conditions - monday.com Legal Portal
    If you still don’t find the answers there, we will be happy to help!

Thanks!
Or Fridman

posts.author: OrFrid (Or Fridman)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Julio!
It was tested mainly in English, but should work also in Spanish.
We welcome you to test the features once you get access to them and share with us your feedback about the quality in Spanish!
Thanks
Or Fridman

posts.author: TFaust (Timo Faust)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @OrFrid ,

thanks for coming back to me and providing me the requested information.

Have a great day.

posts.cookedHtml: Where does the data that the monday AI scrubs come from? AI is notorious for taking writing from people without consent. Have the people who wrote the data that is being scrubbed fully consented to being used by artificial intelligence?

posts.author: Mick_B (Michael Blacklock)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thank you so much for continuing to move the product forward with such innovative ideas.

With regards to the AI functionality, and please excuse my lack of tech understanding; does this functionality open up the data to places like ChatGPT (or whatever AI app is leveraged)?

E.G, we choose a 3rd party app in Monday, that app leverages another AI app (?), this means AI can learn from the data from within Monday - correct?

The concerns are around company / customer privacy.

posts.author: rosaly (Rose @ Sayn)
posts.cookedHtml: Go to your Menu, select monday.labs and scroll down to where it says “Ai Assistant beta” and then select Deactivate. You can activate or deactivate any features you may not like there.

posts.author: rosaly (Rose @ Sayn)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is the Terms and Conditions for the AI. In section 3, it discusses your concern.

They also state on the website overviewing it that “We are using multiple AI services. The specific services that we’re using are proprietary.” You can find more information for it on this page.

posts.author: Runy (Runy)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi
Questions:

Can you use AI models like Chatgpt to access Monday?

I’m using large language models like Chatgpt in python.

Another question I have is this: when doing a discovery call with clients I map their basic processes.

I collect the steps of the process, the fields, the automations and the formulas.

Then to do a demo call I have to mannually create the whole workflow. And then demo it to clients.

This takes some time, and you are not earning any money. This is just a sales process. If you have 10 potential clients and you create 10 demos it costs you a lot of time. And this is a repetitive process that can be automated. By using AI.

It would be great if you could feed the AI with your written description of the paper process, the fields and automations, and the AI produces Code. And that code could be uploaded in Monday and you get the automated process.

Is anybody thinking about this? Anybody knows what the code behind a workflow looks like?

When you create a board with 10 groups and each group has 10 tasks and 20 fields, how does the code look like in the back end?

Is this javascript? How is graphQL used?

I want to experiment with letting the AI bot/chatgpt AI create the code of the board, automations and fields.

Anybody has some thoughts where to start with this?

I can program in python and learning javascript.

So basically: if you hand the requirements of a whole paper workflow to somebody who can program on the platform of Monday (in the back end) they could produce code and then your requirements are turned into a workflow, right?

So I believe the AI can also produce code for the Monday back end. This is what I want to dive in.
